tion ( "setTimeout" , instance, timeout ) ; [$[Get Code]] Use this one commonHost and setTimeout etc. Edward suggested that we can define setTimeout in commonHost and keep track of requests in commonHost. Then, to stop an accessor, we cancel the time

ost. Then, to stop an accessor, we cancel the timeouts and intervals. Differences with Cape Code Cape Code has two JavaScript globals: actor and accessor that are set to the JavaScript actor. capeCodeHost.js invokes actor.setTimeout() etc. However,

Host.js invokes actor.setTimeout() etc. However, in Nashorn, we don't have an actor. For Nashorn, if actor is null, then we could have if ( typeof actor === 'undefined' || actor === null ) { var actor = require ( 'js-timeout-polyfill.js' ) ; fi [$[G
